Bookkeeping logic is on critical path even when async scheduling is turned on. And we found the overhead of bookkeeping is mainly coming from tensor operations.

enable_prompt_embeds will make input_ids argument to be None instead of tensor type, which will invalidate the compile cache at vllm level. Previously this wasn't an issue because inductor has its own caching validation that serves as the last line of defence.
Now that w

vllm.title: [Hybrid] Add mamba_block_size to Engine Args
vllm.author: Josephasafg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Now that prefix caching is enabled, we want the users to be able to control the size of the mamba_block_size to better customize it to their use-case.
At the moment, this flag can only be used with --enable-prefix-caching, as without it we allocate it in size of max_model_len for backwards c

vllm.title: [compile] Disable dynamo guards check for AOT compilation.
vllm.author: zhxchen17
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
Fixing issue #27286
In AOT compilation, we turn on dynamo guards check by default. While they can be useful in general sense, in VLLM they mostly generate noise and cause compilation failure when there shouldn't be compile or recompiles. For example, I saw some CI failures over when we are

This causes a delay in the reporting of KV events from the offloading connector.

This does not yet cover prompt logprobs, only output logprobs.

Purpose
#23207 Broke Qwen3VL since it relies on Qwen2_5_VisionAttention as well but still used the old signature:
RuntimeError: vllm::flash_attn_maxseqlen_wrapper() Expected a value of type 'Tensor' for argument 'max_seqlen' but instead found type 'int'

This PR updates Qwen3VL and Qwen3Omni to fix
